title: Entrance
overview: On his return from a trip to Japan, Oscar Claudel brings a young girl named Yune to work at his metalworks shop, the Enseignes du Roy.

title: Cheese
overview: Yune eats her first French breakfast and attempts to hide her dislike of cheese! Claude, Oscar's grandson, takes Yune shopping.

title: Japanese Labyrinth
overview: Claude becomes inspired by Yune with an idea for a sign for a nearby music shop. Later, they find the owners of Yune's kimono.

title: Water Light
overview: Alice, the youngest of the upper-class Blanche family, becomes interested in Yune and wants her to stay with her, but Yune has made a promise to the Claudels.

title: Lost
overview: Claude scolds Yune for her kindness, which is taken advantage of by a boy who steals from the shop.

title: Crinoline
overview: Alice pays a visit to Yune at Oscar's shop and invites her over for a photograph. There, Yune learns of a past between Alice's sister Camille and Claude

title: Skylight
overview: Yune faints from a fever, which fills Claude with guilt for scolding her earlier. He receives help from Alice and her doctor to cure Yune.

title: A Child's Room
overview: A tea party at the Blanche's brings Claude and Camille together, while Alice shows Yune the nursery where she grew up.

title: Secrets
overview: Claude speaks about the past with Camille when they used to play together in the Blanche's family garden.

title: Phantasmagoria
overview: Oscar spends time with Yune and Alice. He shows off a slide projector that reminds Alice of her parts of her life back in Japan.

title: A Prayer
overview: Alice invites Yune to an exhibition at the Grand Magasin, but Claude opposes and makes other plans for them. Yune shares more about her family in Japan.

title: Cat on the Roof
overview: Yune goes out to search for a lost black cat and gets lost, which worries Claude who cannot find her.
